chunk_summary: This section outlines the micronutrient supplementation schedule for inpatient care of children with severe acute malnutrition (SAM), focusing on infection management and metabolic corrections.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of micronutrient supplementation in managing SAM in inpatient settings.
Learn the specific guidelines for administering vitamins and minerals to children with SAM.
Recognize the importance of infection management and metabolic stabilization in SAM care.
key_facts: Children with SAM require daily supplementation of vitamin A, zinc, and folic acid as per WHO guidelines.
Iron supplementation is delayed until the child’s appetite improves and infection is controlled, typically after 2 weeks.
Vitamin A is administered at a dose of 100,000 IU for children aged 6–12 months and 200,000 IU for those older than 12 months.
Zinc supplementation is critical for immune function and is given at 2 mg/kg/day.
Folic acid is provided at an initial dose of 5 mg, followed by 1 mg daily.
reference_quote: Children with severe acute malnutrition should receive vitamin A supplementation immediately upon admission, as per WHO guidelines. (Source: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK154454/)
